extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is violated. opted_out can only become true via t1 (optout), which also sets sent:=false, and once true it never reverts. The only transition that sets sent:=true while opted_out is true is t5 (send_attempt, guard opted_out and ticks>=4). By advancing ticks to 4 with four tick events, then opting out, then issuing a send_attempt, t5's guard (opted_out and ticks>=4) holds and sets sent:=true while opted_out remains true. Trace: (0)opted_out=F,sent=F,ticks=0; tick->ticks=1; tick->ticks=2; tick->ticks=3; tick->ticks=4; optout->opted_out=T,sent=F,ticks=4; send_attempt-> t5 enabled -> sent=T, opted_out=T. State opted_out=T AND sent=T violates the property. This occurs in 6 events, within the 8-event bound. Note t3 (also enabled here, sets sent:=false) does not save the property: no priority is specified, so t5 is a reachable choice; and since t5 is listed last, any last-listed/highest-index tie-break makes the violation deterministic.
